{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks which muscle does not insert into the greater tubercle of the humerus. From the provided documents, the rotator cuff muscles are the supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor, and subscapularis.

Document [0] states: 'The supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres minor all arise from the posterior scapula and insert onto the greater tubercle of the humerus. The subscapularis arises from the subscapular fossa of the anterior scapula and inserts on the lesser tubercle of the humerus.'

Document [12] confirms: 'The remainder of the rotator cuff muscles (supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres minor) insert into the greater tubercle.'

Document [3] and [23] also state that the subscapularis inserts on the lesser tubercle of the humerus.

Therefore, the subscapularis is the only muscle among the four that does not insert into the greater tubercle of the humerus. The other three (supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor) do insert into the greater tubercle.

Thus, the correct answer is D: Subscapularis.", "answer_choice": "D"}
